summary.verdict_one_liner: The frontend planning bundle is strong, but the executable factory path bypasses it and emits a different app contract.

checks.name: Required factory handoff bundle exists
checks.details: frontend_factory_developer requires a planning/frontend-factory bundle with factory-frontend.json, frontend-contract.json, experience-contract.json, retrieval-manifest.json, scope-manifest.json, roots.json, and scopes/*.json. The solar run only has the stable planning/frontend bundle.

checks.name: DOC and .github mirror stay in sync
checks.details: The public mirror drifted after the no-hardcoded-UI rule hardening. Governance drift is not the primary runtime blocker, but it weakens the source-of-truth contract.

checks.name: Factory planner/developer contract is documented
checks.evidence: Grep:planning/frontend-factory|scope-manifest.json|roots.json in DOC/agents/** → 26 matches across frontend_factory_planner.agent.md and frontend_factory_developer.agent.md

checks.name: Factory runner input schema matches developer contract
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\scripts\run-factory.mjs → parseArgs accepts only brief, runId, clean
checks.details: The executable runner has no planning_root, factory_context, scope packet, or target_mode input. It cannot consume the packet-driven handoff required by frontend_factory_developer.

checks.name: Brief taxonomy normalizes into route-selection taxonomy
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\DOC\output\runs\2026-05-11-solar-installer\planning\frontend\brief.json → productType=marketing_site; constraints.no_auth=true; Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\core-engineering\engine\product-requirement-analyzer.mjs → defaultRoutesForProductType only handles local-services
checks.details: The brief uses marketing_site and local_service semantics, but the analyzer only branches on local-services. no_auth is also ignored, so auth routes remain eligible even when planning explicitly says no_auth.

checks.name: Executable factory code references planner handoff artifacts
checks.details: The standalone execution code does not invoke or consume the documented factory planner/developer protocol. Only README/CLEANUP documentation mentions frontend-factory artifacts.

checks.name: Runtime builder is scope-driven instead of hardcoded
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\builders\engine\frontend-build-plan.mjs → fixed components include ThemeSwitcher, MobileBottomNav, AuthModal; Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\builders\engine\frontend-builder.mjs#route maps → hardcoded labels and pages for /product, /pricing, /sign-in, /sign-up
checks.details: The generator emits a fixed route/component family and then conditionally writes files from a predetermined template set. It is not using allowed_outputs from scope packets or stable page/component specs.

checks.name: Planned route inventory reconciles to emitted runtime routes
checks.details: The stable solar planning bundle has 17 page briefs. The emitted solar runtime app has only 6 page.tsx routes and overlaps on only home and contact.

checks.name: Planned component families appear in runtime implementation
checks.evidence: Run:component presence check in emitted solar src → ROICalculator=missing, BeforeAfterSlider=missing, WhatsAppButton=missing, ServiceAreaMap=missing, PortfolioCard=missing, TestimonialCard=missing
checks.details: The runtime app does not implement core plan-specific solar components even though the planning bundle specifies them.

checks.name: Two-run determinism audit
checks.evidence: Run:not executed → this audit focused on source-of-truth alignment rather than hash comparison
checks.details: The generator appears deterministic, but deterministic wrong output is still wrong output. The binding issue is protocol bypass, not output randomness.

checks.name: Release gate measures plan-to-runtime alignment
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\validators\engine\production-readiness.mjs → checks only generated artifact existence, route counts, hero uniqueness, and mandatory UX
checks.details: The production-readiness validator never compares emitted runtime routes/components against the locked solar planning bundle. A generic app can pass the release gate while violating the plan.

checks.name: Generated E2E suite evaluates planned journeys
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\builders\engine\frontend-builder.mjs#tests → smoke/full tests assert auth modal plus /sign-in and /sign-up routes
checks.details: The test suite validates the generator's internal generic contract instead of the planner's declared journeys. This makes E2E green without proving solar-plan compliance.

checks.name: Plan-aware bridge smoke fixture exists
checks.details: The stable solar planning bundle does not include the factory-native execution contracts the developer protocol expects, and the standalone factory runner has no bridge smoke fixture for stable frontend planning.

I.name: Frontend quality bar and creative latitude

checks.name: Emitted runtime preserves planned route semantics
checks.details: The solar brand and summary were injected into a generic route system. This is not a solar-specific route architecture and does not preserve the planner's page inventory.

checks.name: Emitted content preserves planned component and proposition logic
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\ai-product-factory\generated\apps\solar-fresh-2026-05-12\sunenergy-pro\src\lib\site-content.ts → generic factory copy remains ('Composition, not copy-paste templates', 'Factory V2 preview', 'Review packages')
checks.details: The runtime content mixes solar brand fields with generic factory marketing propositions. This is a composition fallback, not a faithful implementation of the locked planner output.

checks.name: frontend_factory_developer preconditions are enforced before execution
checks.evidence: Read:f:\PROJECTS\Agent\DOC\agents\frontend_factory_developer.agent.md → planning_root must exist and point to planning/frontend-factory; Run:Test-Path planning/frontend-factory → False
checks.details: By contract, execution should have blocked immediately. Instead, the runnable path accepted brief.json and generated a fresh but protocol-incompatible app.

checks.name: Required developer audit artifacts are emitted
checks.evidence: FileSearch:ai-product-factory/generated/apps/solar-fresh-2026-05-12/sunenergy-pro/.audit/** → 0 files; Grep:frontend-self-audit|frontend-factory-manifest|.audit in ai-product-factory/builders/** → writes reports/frontend-self-audit.json only
checks.details: The documented execution contract requires .audit/frontend-self-audit.md and .audit/frontend-factory-manifest.json. The generator emits reports/frontend-self-audit.json instead, so the completion contract is incomplete.

inconsistencies.description: Executable runner consumes brief-only input instead of factory_context plus scoped planning packets.
marketing_site/local_service brief semantics do not normalize into the route taxonomy used by the analyzer.
Emitted runtime route set diverges from the locked solar page inventory.
Runtime audit output path does not match the documented .audit contract.

fixes.exact_change: Replace brief-only execution with a required handoff mode selector. Add --planning-root, --target-mode, and --factory-context arguments. Block when planning_root does not contain factory-frontend.json, frontend-contract.json, experience-contract.json, retrieval-manifest.json, scope-manifest.json, and scopes/*.json.

Normalize brief product taxonomy and constraints. Map marketing_site plus local_service or businessType=local_service to the local-service route family, and suppress auth/auth-modal/sign-in/sign-up defaults when constraints.no_auth == true.
Derive routes, components, required checks, and release journeys from planner contracts and scope packets instead of the fixed ThemeSwitcher/MobileBottomNav/AuthModal/Product/Pricing baseline.

Replace the hardcoded route/page writers with packet-driven emitters. Only emit files listed in scope.allowed_outputs and map them from stable planner source_files. Remove unconditional product/pricing/auth templates from the baseline emitter.
fixes.blast_radius: ai-product-factory/builders/engine/frontend-builder.mjs, generated app snapshots, generated test files

fixes.exact_change: Add plan-to-runtime validation. Compare locked route inventory, required component families, mandatory UX exceptions, and expected audit outputs against the emitted app root before returning passed.
Generate Playwright smoke/full/a11y journeys from planner e2e-journeys.json or stable route coverage bridge data. Remove fixed auth-modal smoke assertions when auth is not in scope.

Add a required bridge mode that can transform a stable planning/frontend bundle into a factory-native planning/frontend-factory bundle. Declare this bridge mandatory before frontend_factory_developer may run against a stable planner output.

Add an explicit block for stable planning/frontend bundles presented without a corresponding frontend-factory bridge bundle. Require the developer to stop instead of falling back to brief-only generation.
Sync the public mirror with the DOC source, including the new no-hardcoded-UI rules and any added block conditions.
Add an optional plan-alignment verification step before npm run release:check, and install the full Playwright browser set when the generated suite uses WebKit or Firefox.

contract_mismatches: Generated routes and tests followed the standalone fallback contract, not the locked solar plan.

verdict.reason: The stable solar planner output is not the problem. The blocking defect is that the executable factory path does not consume the planner's contract. It accepts brief-only input, hardcodes a generic route/component/test model, and therefore can generate a fresh app that passes its own gates while violating the planned site.
verdict.next_action: Apply fix #1 first: make the runner consume a factory-native planning bundle or block. Then re-audit route coverage and plan-aware release checks before any new site build.
